description: An access-master configuration row (RA RoutingRuleEntry) that maps a department and an approval stage (vertical) to the pool of users who can be assigned the stage work and the pool who can approve it, plus the per-approver auto-approval limits. The approval engine resolves a record's ordered chain by matching routing rules on department/category/recordType within a value band. One engine, parameterized entirely by these rows; Al Bahja all-to-one and Raphe multi-stage are both just different sets of routing rules. There is exactly one rule per (department, stage).

fields.name: ruleId
fields.label: Routing Rule No.
fields.purpose: Stable key for one routing-rule row so audit entries and resolved-chain references attach to a specific configuration; lets an Administrator edit or retire a rule without re-keying.

fields.name: departmentId
fields.label: Department
fields.validation: a configured business unit/vertical; part of the unique key (department, stage)
fields.purpose: The department/vertical this rule routes for; the approval engine matches a record's departmentId against this when resolving the chain, so a record's department determines which approver pools apply.

fields.name: stage
fields.label: Stage / Vertical
fields.validation: one of the configured chain stages (verticalId); unique per (departmentId, stage); chain order is fixed REQ_DEPARTMENT -> PROCUREMENT -> BUDGET -> FINANCE -> MANAGEMENT, stages are individually enable-able
fields.purpose: The stage of the approval chain this rule configures; determines the routing strategy (REQ_DEPARTMENT pinned to requester, FINANCE/MANAGEMENT approval-only) and which approver pool the engine reads for that stage.

fields.name: category
fields.label: Category Scope
fields.validation: one of the enum, or null for all categories; narrows the match alongside departmentId and recordType
fields.purpose: Optional category filter so a department can route Items/Spares/Services/ProductDesign through different approver pools; null means the rule applies to every category in the department.

fields.name: recordType
fields.label: Record Type Scope
fields.validation: one of the set; the engine matches on (departmentId, category, recordType); null applies to all record types
fields.purpose: Scopes the rule to a record kind so one engine can route requisitions, POs, sourcing awards, supplier onboarding, and payment schedules with different pools per stage.

fields.name: valueBandMin
fields.label: Value Band From
fields.validation: >= 0; base currency; lower bound (inclusive) of the totalAmountInBase band this rule applies to
fields.purpose: Lets a department route low-value and high-value buys through different chains; the engine selects the rule whose band contains the record's totalAmountInBase (axiom A12).

fields.name: valueBandMax
fields.label: Value Band To
fields.validation: >= valueBandMin; base currency; null = no upper bound; upper bound (inclusive) of the totalAmountInBase band
fields.purpose: Upper edge of the value band; together with valueBandMin defines the spend range that selects this rule, so high-value buys can require a longer chain.

fields.name: assigneeUserIds
fields.label: Assignee Pool
fields.mandatoryCondition: required for assignable stages (not FINANCE/MANAGEMENT which are approval-only)
fields.validation: active users; least-loaded selection draws the stage assignee from this pool; empty for FINANCE/MANAGEMENT (axiom A5); empty pool on an assignable stage raises 'No eligible approver'
fields.purpose: The candidate users the engine assigns the stage's work to (least-loaded, urgency-weighted). REQ_DEPARTMENT ignores this and pins to the requester; FINANCE/MANAGEMENT leave it empty because they are approval-only (axiom A5).

fields.name: approverUserIds
fields.label: Approver Pool
fields.validation: active users with permission approval.approve; nearest-bucket + least-loaded selection draws the approver from this pool; each must satisfy SoD at resolve time (!= record requester/buyer, axiom A6); empty pool raises BadRequestException 'No eligible approver' and the record stalls
fields.purpose: The candidate approvers for the stage; the engine filters by limit (nearest bucket), SoD, and load to pick the one approver who must decide. The pool, not a single named approver, is what keeps the chain resilient when people are absent.

fields.name: approverLimits
fields.label: Per-Approver Limits
fields.validation: map of userId -> limit (base currency); a missing entry falls back to DEFAULT_LIMIT 200000; a configured 0 stays 0 and never falls back; >= 0
fields.purpose: The per-approver auto-approval ceiling used by nearest-bucket selection and the auto-approve guard (amountInBase <= limit, axiom A7). Per-approver because the engine reserves high-limit approvers for large amounts; a 0 forces manual review for any positive amount.

fields.name: allowAutoApproval
fields.label: Allow Auto-Approval
fields.validation: when false the stage always routes to a human even within limit; MANAGEMENT is terminal and never auto-approves regardless
fields.purpose: Per-stage switch that gates the within-threshold auto-approval path (axiom A6/A7); lets a department force a human decision on a stage even when the amount is under limit.

fields.name: requiredDesignationRank
fields.label: Required Designation Rank
fields.validation: >= 0; a manual reassignment requires the assigner's designation rank >= this; an assignee may approve only if its rank meets this
fields.purpose: Minimum seniority for this stage; gates manual reassignment and lets a sufficiently senior assignee act as approver. Encodes the designation-rank check the assignment and approval guards enforce.

fields.name: siteCondition
fields.label: Site / Conditional Rule
fields.validation: optional predicate, e.g. {includeSites:[...], excludeSites:[...]}; when present the stage is created only for matching sites/departments
fields.purpose: Models a site-conditional stage such as the Al Bahja Factory Manager rule (FM sites Barka Engineering, Oman Hygienic Stores/Engineering vs FM-skip sites Oman Solar, Accommodation Maintenance, Flavour Division, Photon-01); the engine skips the stage where the predicate excludes the site.

fields.name: isActive
fields.label: Active
fields.validation: inactive rules are ignored by chain resolution; deactivating a rule that leaves a department/stage uncovered will stall records with 'No routing rule'
fields.purpose: Soft-disable a rule without deleting it (preserving the audit trail); an inactive rule drops out of resolution so the engine raises 'No routing rule' if nothing else covers the department/stage.

invariants: A2 the resolved chain advances one stage at a time
A5 Finance/Management stages are approval-only (no assigneeUserIds); an APPROVED stage cannot be reassigned
A6 SoD: a resolved approver is never the record's requester/buyer; auto-approval (allowAutoApproval) is the only no-second-person path and is logged
A7 auto-approval requires amountInBase <= the per-approver limit (default 200000)
A12 value-band match and limit comparison use totalAmountInBase
A13 every rule change is audited; missing/inactive coverage raises 'No routing rule' and stalls the record
